name: Single Owner Chunks give each Ethereum account a virtual namespace of writable slots

links.targetTitle: Swarm exposes two primitives: immutable content-addressed /bytes and mutable owner-signed feeds

global.title: Single Owner Chunks give each Ethereum account a virtual namespace of writable slots
global.content:
## Claim

A **Single Owner Chunk (SOC)** is a chunk whose address is derived from an identifier and an owner Ethereum address, giving every account a private virtual namespace of writable chunk slots.

## Evidence

- Address formula: `Keccak256(identifier || owner_address)` — deterministic from a 32-byte identifier and the owner's Ethereum address
- Content layout: 32-byte identifier + 65-byte signature (r, s, v) + span + payload
- Only the private-key holder can sign updates; anyone can read
- A SOC can **wrap** a CAC — the SOC payload becomes the CAC's span + data
- The addressing scheme means each account controls what lives at each identifier — the primitive underneath feeds and GSOC

## Why it matters

SOCs are the primitive that turns Swarm from immutable content storage into a system that supports mutable, signed state. Feeds and GSOC are both built on top of SOCs.
global.description: SOC address = Keccak256(identifier || owner_address). Only the owner's private key can write; anyone can read. Basis for feeds and GSOC.
